name: Vindaloo
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Dried hot red chili flakes
4 Garlic cloves, ground to a paste
1 lb Pork, boneless, cut into 2-inch cubes
1 lb Beef, boneless, cut into 2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
Vinegar is a preservative and the vindaloo may be kept in the refrigerator for several days before using. The flavors are intensified if the vindaloo is eaten one or two days before cooking, warmed slightly.
Toast the coriander, poppy, mustard seeds, and cumin seeds in a dry skillet for 2-3 minutes until the aroma is released. Grind the seeds to a fine powder and set aside.
Heat oil in a pan and add all the toasted dry spices, chili flakes, ginger, and garlic. Stir fry over moderately low heat for 2 minutes. Add the meat and stir continuously for 10 minutes as it browns. Add water and cover the pan. Cook until the meat is tender, about 1 hour.
When the meat has been fully tenderized, add vinegar and continue to cook until the vinegar evaporates and the oil rises to the surface. This indicates that the vindaloo is ready. Pour off any excess oil before serving. Serve warm with plain white rice and masoor dal.
SERVES: 6
